LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS EDWARD OLSHANETSKY AND BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS LIMITED TO CONTRACTUAL REMEDIES. OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 AS TO BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT CONTROLLED THE DISPOSITION OF THE COMMISSION. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO OLSHANETSKY WHO IS NOT A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS EDWARD OLSHANETSKY, BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1, TO PLEAD EITHER FACTS SUPPORTING A FIDUCIARY DUTY OR A CLAIM BASED ON THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 TO ATTACH THE CONTRACT OR PLEAD THE LANGUAGE OF THE TERM ALLEGEDLY BREACHED. Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS EDWARD OLSHANETSKY AND BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 1 because plaintiff is limited to contractual remedies. Overruled as to cause of action 2 as to Business Investment Management because plaintiff has alleged that defendant controlled the disposition of the commission. Sustained without leave to amend as to Olshanetsky who is not a party to the contract. Moving party to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J.
